name: Alto de Codos Loop from Cariñena
name_original: Cariñena - Codos - Pietas - Codos - Aguarón - Encinacorba — Sierra de Algairén
description: This difficult 58.7-mile road cycling loop from Cariñena offers challenging climbs, 5357 feet of elevation gain, and scenic views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Cariñena is characterized by its challenging hilly terrain and significant elevation changes, offering routes that traverse varied landscapes. The region features extensive vineyards and open countryside, providing a backdrop for demanding rides. Cyclists can expect long climbs and rewarding descents through the Aragonese pre-Pyrenees foothills. The area's topography makes it suitable for experienced road cyclists seeking endurance challenges. Best road cycling routes around Cariñena The most popular road cycling route is Alto de Codos Loop from Cariñena, a 58.7 miles (94.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de Codos – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Eagle loop from Cariñena, a difficult 55.7 miles (89.6 km) path. This route offers winding roads and panoramic views across the region. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Codos – ️ Church of San Pedro loop from Cariñena, a 49.0 miles (78.9 km) trail leading through rolling hills and open countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Road cycling around Cariñena is defined by challenging climbs, open countryside, and vineyard landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy road bike trails Cariñena, though many routes feature significant elevation. The routes in Cariñena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cariñena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cariñena?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Cariñena, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region is known for its challenging hilly terrain and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes in Cariñena for beginners?
A: While Cariñena is characterized by challenging hilly terrain, there are 3 routes specifically rated as easy. These routes offer a gentler introduction to road cycling in the region, though many routes feature significant elevation.

Q: What kind of distances and elevation gains can I expect on road cycling routes in Cariñena?
A: Road cycling routes in Cariñena typically range from approximately 50 to over 80 miles (80 to 130 km) in length. You can expect substantial elevation gains, often exceeding 3,000 feet (1,000 meters) and sometimes reaching over 6,000 feet (1,800 meters) on longer routes, reflecting the region's mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Cariñena?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40338771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Codos – Santa Cruz de Grío loop from Cariñena&lt;/a&gt; offer demanding climbs and rewarding descents. This route covers approximately 68 miles (109 km) with over 5,900 feet (1,800 meters) of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Cariñena?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Cariñena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988409048/alto-de-codos-loop-from-carinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Codos Loop from Cariñena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Codos – Hermitage of the Virgin of the Eagle loop from Cariñena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Cariñena?
A: Along the routes, you'll encounter extensive vineyards and open countryside. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Eagle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also offer panoramic views across the Aragonese pre-Pyrenees foothills.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Cariñena?
A: The best time for road biking in Cariñena is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Cariñena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, the scenic vineyard landscapes, and the rewarding descents through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any specific scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views, particularly as you traverse the hilly terrain and mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Luz&lt;/a&gt; is a notable mountain pass that provides expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling trails in Cariñena?
A: While many routes are well-known, exploring the network can reveal less-trafficked roads. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Codos – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Cariñena&lt;/a&gt;, though challenging, takes you through diverse landscapes that might offer a sense of discovery beyond the most popular trails.

Q: Is there public transport access to the road cycling routes in Cariñena?
A: Information regarding specific public transport access directly to the start points of these road cycling routes is not readily available. Most cyclists typically travel to Cariñena by car and park there to begin their rides.

Q: Where can I park my car when road cycling in Cariñena?
A: Cariñena, as a town, generally offers various parking options. It is advisable to look for public parking areas within the town center or near the start of your chosen route. Specific parking details for individual trailheads are not provided, so planning ahead for town parking is recommended.
tips: Located in the beautiful town of Codos, we find this Renaissance-style temple, built in the 16th century and renovated during the 18th century.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Alto de Codos Loop from Cariñena for cyclists?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, requiring very good fitness due to significant climbs and substantial elevation gain. It's considered a demanding ride and a test of endurance, attracting cyclists looking for a physical challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly well-paved, typical for road cycling. However, be aware that some segments on certain variations of this loop may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Alto de Codos Loop?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points. You'll climb to the Alto de Codos, a significant mountain pass. Other highlights include the historic ⭐️ Church of San Pedro and the convenient Pietas Fountain and Picnic Area.

faqs.question: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, cyclists can expect picturesque views, particularly once at the top of the Alto de Codos. From this vantage point, a large part of the Campo de Cariñena is visible, offering immersive views of the Spanish countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Alto de Codos Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the region around Cariñena is known for its challenging road cycling. Generally, spring and autumn offer more moderate temperatures for demanding rides like this, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential colder conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The tour starts from Cariñena. While specific parking details aren't provided, Cariñena is a town, so you should be able to find public parking options within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra de Algairén area?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Sierra de Algairén. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads in this region of Spain. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de Codos Loop from Cariñena suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route on public roads, which is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs, especially given its difficult rating and significant distance. For dog-friendly activities, consider dedicated walking or hiking trails where dogs are explicitly permitted and can be safely managed.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections of [CIMA ZA03] Codos - Valdemadera * Cariñena, Derivación 2: Tobed - Cariñena, Etapa 6: Morata de Jalón - Tobed, and the GR90.2, among others, along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Cariñena, a town that offers various services. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options there to refuel or stay overnight before or after your challenging ride.
